title: Fix blank web shell home route
description: Home route in apps/web can fail before rendering because the wallet provider is mounted without React Query context. Update the shell app wiring so the root page renders reliably and keep a regression test for /.
close_reason: Completed

title: Initialize RFQ/API worker, D1/DO, and broadcast worker without product features
description:
## What to build
Create the baseline Cloudflare worker applications and shared infrastructure for the RFQ/API server, Durable Objects, D1 database, queues, and broadcast worker. Keep product workflows out of scope for this setup gate.

## Blocked by
None - can start immediately
design: This is a HITL setup gate, not a product feature slice.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] RFQ/API worker and broadcast worker have local boot, config, and health endpoint foundations
- [ ] D1 schema baseline, Durable Object baseline, and queue baseline exist
- [ ] Later product slices can add quote, execution, dashboard, and settlement logic without reworking the worker foundation
close_reason: Completed

title: Initialize web UI application without product features
description:
## What to build
Create the mobile-first web UI shell for the options trading protocol with shared routing, wallet session shell, loading and error boundaries, and shared UI primitives. Keep product workflows out of scope for this setup gate.

## Blocked by
None - can start immediately
design: This is a HITL setup gate, not a product feature slice.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A standalone web UI app boots locally with routing, shared layout, and config wiring
- [ ] Wallet session shell, loading states, and error states exist without product feature screens
- [ ] Later taker and maker slices can be added without reworking the app foundation
notes: use vanila react + vite https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/framework-guides/web-apps/react/ app that can be deployed as a cloduflare worker and/or cloduflare page
close_reason: Completed

title: options: preserve underwriting public API names
description: Replace the newly introduced underwrite_call_signed and underwrite_put_signed endpoints with signed OrderV1 requirements on the existing underwrite_call and underwrite_put public entry points. Keep shared implementation in private/package helpers, preserve non-composability, and update tests so no extra signed-suffix public API remains.
design: Preserve the established API names from the specification. Convert them to non-composable entry functions and move internal asset-transfer logic into non-public helpers.
acceptance_criteria: Existing underwrite_call and underwrite_put are the only public underwriting entry points; both require SignedOrderV1 and BuyerVault and enforce all current signature, replay, vault debit, collateral, fee, transfer, and accounting checks; underwrite_call_signed and underwrite_put_signed are removed; call/put success and rejection tests pass; sui move test passes.
close_reason: Restored the specified underwriting entry-point names with signed OrderV1 behavior preserved

title: options: migrate series call/put markers from 0/1 to 1/2
description: Update packages/options/sources/series.move so call uses 1 and put uses 2 instead of 0 and 1. This avoids inconsistency with the updated options spec and other parts of the system that now use 1 for call and 2 for put. Review related validations, stored markers, tests, and any public interfaces that rely on the old encoding.
close_reason: Migrated call/put markers to spec-defined 1/2 encoding with regression coverage

title: options: implement BuyerVault closure
description: Implement BuyerVault closure according to spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md. The vault owner closes a BuyerVault<QuoteCoin> by consuming the shared vault, withdrawing its full currently available Balance<QuoteCoin>, deleting the vault UID, and emitting BuyerVaultClosed with vault id, owner, quote coin type, and last withdrawal amount. Preserve the existing owner-only authorization and composable coin-return pattern.
design: Add the close operation and BuyerVaultClosed event to the existing buyer_vault module. Capture event fields before deleting the UID, convert the full Balance<QuoteCoin> to Coin<QuoteCoin>, emit once, and return the coin for PTB composability.
acceptance_criteria: Only ctx.sender() matching the stored vault owner can close the vault; close consumes and deletes the BuyerVault object; the full available QuoteCoin balance, including zero, is returned as Coin<QuoteCoin>; BuyerVaultClosed contains the deleted vault id, owner, quote coin type, and final withdrawn amount; a closed vault cannot be reused; Move tests cover funded close, empty close, unauthorized close, returned value, event fields, and object consumption; sui move test passes.
close_reason: Implemented owner-only BuyerVault closure with full-balance coin return, UID deletion, closure event, and funded/empty/unauthorized/deletion coverage.

title: options: align underwriting implementation with smart-contract specification
description:
The current packages/options/sources/underwriting.move implementation is misaligned with spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md. Fix all findings in one coherent change:

1. Underwritten omits the required long_token_id field. Add the minted Long object ID to the event.
2. underwrite_call and underwrite_put currently return Long, seller premium, and fee, allowing callers to retain or redirect assets while supplying buyer and fee_recipient values that only affect event metadata. Each function must enforce the specified transfers: send Long to buyer, premium_total - operational_fee to the seller, and operational_fee to fee_recipient.
3. Underwriting does not enforce the protocol global pause for already-created series. Add the required pause check so call and put underwriting abort while paused.

Design constraint: underwrite_call and underwrite_put MUST NOT be designed as PTB-composable variants returning assets. We need exactly one production implementation of each operation, and each must perform the required transfers internally according to the specification. Remove or consolidate redundant *_and_transfer wrappers so there is only one authoritative call path per option type.
acceptance_criteria: Underwritten includes long_token_id; underwrite_call internally transfers Long to buyer, net premium to seller, and fee to fee_recipient; underwrite_put does the same; neither function returns those assets for PTB composition; only one production implementation exists for call underwriting and one for put underwriting; both abort under the protocol global pause; Move tests cover recipient transfers, event data, fee rules, and pause behavior; sui move test passes.
close_reason: Human stamp as done/completed after local code review

title: Deepen Series by internalizing CollateralPool custody
description:
Why this exists: callers currently have to pass and coordinate Series plus CollateralPool, so they must know the matching invariant and custody storage shape. This keeps the Series Interface shallow around underwriting, exercise, and seller settlement.

What needs to be done: refactor Series custody so CollateralPool is internal to Series as a stored custody Module/struct, not a separate shared object input. Public underwriting, exercise, and settlement Interfaces must no longer take CollateralPool from callers. Series should remain the external Seam and decide lifecycle facts; CollateralPool should be an internal Implementation Module that handles collateral deposits, exercise proceeds, SellerVault payout allocation, accounted balances, payout calculation, overdraw prevention, and dust/excess accounting.

Verification: this addresses the architectural issue only if CollateralPool disappears from caller-facing Interfaces and tests assert custody outcomes through Series actions.
acceptance_criteria: Public underwriting, exercise, and settlement functions do not accept CollateralPool as an argument; Series owns one internal CollateralPool custody field; tests cover call and put underwriting, manual exercise, seller settlement, custody solvency, overdraw failures, and dust/excess accounting through the Series Interface; spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md describes CollateralPool as internal Series custody and no longer requires a separate shared CollateralPool object or collateral pool id event field.
close_reason: Internalized CollateralPool as UID-bearing Series custody; removed pool from caller APIs/events; added Series custody, dust, solvency, and overdraw coverage.

title: options: implement signed OrderV1 underwriting
description:
Implement signed `OrderV1` underwriting for options as specified in `spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md`.

Replace the direct buyer-premium underwriting path with seller-submitted fills backed by a buyer signature. The implementation should follow the spec for canonical `OrderV1` encoding, signature verification, replay protection, buyer vault debit, seller collateral deposit, premium and fee handling, long mint/transfer, and seller/series accounting for both covered calls and cash-secured puts.

Keep task-level detail minimal here and treat `spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md` as the source of truth for exact field bindings, validation rules, and transaction effects.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Underwriting behavior matches the `Underwriting` and `OrderV1` sections of `spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md` for covered calls and cash-secured puts.
- [ ] `OrderV1` encoding and signature verification match the spec, including canonical BCS serialization and Sui personal-message signing.
- [ ] Successful fills atomically consume replay protection, debit the correct `BuyerVault`, accept exact seller collateral, pay seller and fee recipient, update seller/series accounting, and transfer the minted `Long` to the buyer, as defined in the spec.
- [ ] Invalid or mismatched orders are rejected according to the spec and do not mutate balances, replay state, or position accounting.
- [ ] Move tests cover successful call and put fills and representative rejection paths defined by the spec.
- [ ] `sui move test` passes.
close_reason: Implemented signed OrderV1 underwriting with canonical BCS and Sui personal-message verification, replay protection, BuyerVault debit, call/put fills, and tests

title: Define operator role for options lifecycle
description: The options package currently has AdminCap for market creation and expiry price creation, while series creation and seller settlement are public permissionless calls. Define and implement a distinct operator role if market/series/finalization/settlement should be operator-gated.
notes: Clarification required: the specification explicitly requires permissionless series creation and permissionless seller settlement, so those must not be operator-gated. Please define whether a distinct OperatorCap should replace or complement AdminCap for market creation and expiry-price creation/finalization; specify who creates, grants, revokes, and rotates it; whether it is global or market-scoped; and which exact public functions require it.
close_reason: contradicts the spec, must not be implemented

title: Fix underwriting max operational fee to use market-configured basis points
description: Current underwriting uses max_operational_fee as an optional absolute coin amount, which varies from OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md. Fix the implementation to use a market-configured maximum fee in basis points and reject underwriting when operational_fee exceeds the configured basis-points cap over premium_total.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix web to rfq-server CORS for local dev
description: The web app at http://localhost:5173 calls rfq-server at http://localhost:8787 and browser requests fail because rfq-server does not return CORS headers or handle preflight. Add worker-side CORS handling for local web usage and cover it with tests.

title: Refactor rfq-server to Hono
description: The rfq-server worker has an inline TODO to replace manual request routing in src/index.ts with Hono. Migrate the existing routes to Hono while preserving current API behavior and tests.
close_reason: Completed

title: Refactor rfq-server vault DB helpers
description: The rfq-server app has an inline TODO to move maker vault DB query helpers out of src/index.ts. Extract the D1 query helpers into a dedicated db module and keep behavior unchanged so the worker file is smaller and easier to extend.
close_reason: Completed

title: Build maker positions dashboard vertical
description:
## What to build
Replace the maker positions placeholder with the SPEC.md maker positions view backed by RFQ/API data and chain/oracle state.

## Scope
Show open positions, current ITM/OTM state, required settlement funds, settlement readiness, and settlement history for vaults owned by the connected maker. Keep professional option-trader language. Do not change vault lifecycle flows.
acceptance_criteria: Focused tests prove owned-position loading and rendering of ITM/OTM, required funds, readiness, and history including empty/error states; unauthorized wallet data is not exposed; relevant web and RFQ tests/builds pass.

title: Complete maker vault close flow
description:
## What to build
Replace the pasted-digest close UI with the complete wallet-signed close_vault flow through RFQ broadcast and D1 soft deletion.

## Scope
The maker initiates close from an owned vault, signs and broadcasts the transaction, RFQ verifies the successful OTP_PACKAGE_ID result, then sets deleted_at and enabled=false. Closed vaults cannot be edited or used. Do not implement create, funding, or positions in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Focused tests prove the UI-to-RFQ-to-chain-receipt-to-D1 close flow; invalid package, wrong owner, failed transaction, and repeated close are rejected; closed vault editing/use is blocked; relevant web and RFQ tests/builds pass.

title: Complete maker vault funding flow
description:
## What to build
Add maker dashboard deposit and withdraw actions for an owned vault as one wallet-signed RFQ broadcast vertical.

## Scope
The maker can enter an amount, sign the correct QuoteCoin transaction, broadcast it through RFQ, and see the refreshed on-chain balance. Reject wrong-owner, unsupported-coin, closed-vault, and invalid-amount requests. Do not implement create, close, or positions in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Focused tests cover successful deposit and withdraw plus owner, coin, closed-vault, and amount failures; dashboard balance refreshes from chain after finality; relevant web and RFQ tests/builds pass.

title: Complete maker vault creation flow
description:
## What to build
Complete the maker dashboard create_vault vertical through UI, RFQ broadcast, verified on-chain receipt parsing, and makers_vaults persistence. Reuse the existing supported-coin server configuration and current maker dashboard work.

## Scope
A connected maker selects a supported QuoteCoin and endpoint URLs, signs the create_vault transaction, submits it through the RFQ broadcast path, and sees the newly persisted vault with its chain-read balance. Do not implement deposit, withdraw, close, or positions in this task.
acceptance_criteria: A focused test proves the create flow from web request through RFQ receipt validation and D1 persistence; only OTP_PACKAGE_ID BuyerVault results are trusted; the dashboard refreshes to show the created vault and chain balance; relevant web and RFQ tests/builds pass.
close_reason: Completed maker vault creation flow with queued broadcast, verified receipt persistence, UI signing, and environment wiring

title: Update apps/web to shadcn preset bddBUGsC
description: Switch the apps/web frontend to use shadcn and apply the requested preset bddBUGsC. Inspect the current setup, update config and generated UI pieces as needed, and verify the app still builds/tests.
close_reason: Completed

title: Audit open P0-P2 work for Rust worker wording
description: User updated SPEC.md and asked to check all currently open P0-P2 tasks for stale references to Rust worker implementation details such as Rust, worker-rs, axum, and similar backend-specific wording. Update relevant docs/spec artifacts to use TypeScript wording or remove implementation-specific language where it no longer applies.
close_reason: Audited open P0-P2 work and updated active issue wording to remove Rust-specific backend assumptions.

title: Add just command recipes to worker apps
description: Add casey/just command recipes to both new Cloudflare Worker apps so developers can run dev, test, check, and build commands consistently from each app.
acceptance_criteria: Both worker apps provide working just dev and just test recipes, plus discoverable validation/build recipes; READMEs use the recipes.
close_reason: Added just command recipes for development, tests, checks, builds, and RFQ local migrations; updated both worker READMEs and verified recipes.

title: Run expiry settlement and show final state
description:
## What to build
Build the first expiry settlement path so due positions are selected, settlement jobs are persisted, on-chain settlement transactions are submitted in sequence, and final results appear in taker and maker history. Covered call and cash-secured put must work through the same shared path.

## Blocked by
Depends on the two setup gates, maker readiness, quote acceptance plus submit, and current-state dashboards.
design: AFK slice. Treat covered call and cash-secured put as modes of one capability, not separate implementations. Completion requires touching web UI, RFQ/API, D1 or Durable Objects, and the broadcast service.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A cron or authorized API path can create settlement jobs for due positions and persist them in D1
- [ ] Broadcast service submits settlement transactions in queue order and records success or failure back into storage
- [ ] Taker and maker dashboards show final settled state and history for both covered call and cash-secured put positions

title: See current state on dashboards
description:
## What to build
Build the first read path for both takers and makers so they can see current position state, transaction state, and recent activity for open, pending, expired, exercised, and settled positions. Covered call and cash-secured put must work through the same shared path.

## Blocked by
Depends on the two setup gates, maker readiness, and quote acceptance plus submit.
design: AFK slice. Treat covered call and cash-secured put as modes of one capability, not separate implementations. Completion requires touching web UI, RFQ/API, D1 or Durable Objects, and the broadcast service.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Taker and maker dashboards show current positions, transaction state, and recent activity from shared persisted read models
- [ ] RFQ/API serves dashboard views backed by D1 state that includes strike, premium, current or expiry price, and state labels
- [ ] Broadcast service writes transaction lifecycle updates back into storage so dashboards reflect live progress and final outcomes

title: Accept quote and submit underwrite
description:
## What to build
Build the end-to-end execution path where a taker accepts a live quote, the protocol re-checks validity, fetches and verifies the maker order, persists the execution record, and submits the underwrite transaction on-chain. Covered call and cash-secured put must work through the same shared path.

## Blocked by
Depends on the two setup gates, maker readiness, and quote request.
design: AFK slice. Treat covered call and cash-secured put as modes of one capability, not separate implementations. Completion requires touching web UI, RFQ/API, D1 or Durable Objects, and the broadcast service.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The web UI can accept a live quote and show pending, submitted, confirmed, or failed execution state
- [ ] RFQ/API re-checks quote validity and remaining size, fetches and verifies the maker order, and stores the execution record in D1
- [ ] Broadcast service queues and submits the underwrite transaction and writes lifecycle status back for the UI to read

title: Request quote
description:
## What to build
Build the first taker quote request flow for supported markets. The taker chooses asset, strategy, strike, expiry, and size, and receives the best live quote with premium, collateral, timer, and simple outcome preview. Covered call and cash-secured put must work through the same shared path.

## Blocked by
Depends on the two setup gates and maker readiness.
design: AFK slice. Treat covered call and cash-secured put as modes of one capability, not separate implementations. Completion requires touching web UI, RFQ/API, D1 or Durable Objects, and the broadcast service.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The web UI quote builder supports supported assets and both covered call and cash-secured put in one shared flow
- [ ] RFQ/API fans out to eligible makers, verifies signed quotes, and stores quote state, expiry, and remaining capacity in D1 or Durable Objects
- [ ] Broadcast service provides transaction readiness data for the selected market so the returned quote is actionable, not just informational

title: Maker onboarding and eligibility readiness
description:
## What to build
Build the first maker onboarding slice around maker vault setup and RFQ registration without implementing quote or buy-order API verification yet.

This slice should cover hidden maker dashboard visibility, vault visibility, wallet-signed vault create/deposit/withdraw/close flows, maker-managed quote and order endpoint URL configuration, and admin CLI control of maker vault enable or disable state in the RFQ database.

Quote endpoint verification, order endpoint verification, quote signature verification, and order signature verification are explicitly out of scope for this task and must be handled by future tasks.
design:
AFK slice. Completion should touch maker web UI and RFQ/API persistence. Broadcast-side vault eligibility checks are out of scope for this task.

Web app submits maker vault transactions through the RFQ server broadcast path defined by the spec. The exact request/response shape for create_vault, deposit, withdraw, and close_vault may be designed during implementation as the simplest workable server contract for this slice.

RFQ stores maker vault metadata defined by SPEC.md in the makers_vaults table, including the id, owner, and quote_coin_type fields of the on-chain BuyerVault struct, reads balances from chain on the web app side, allows makers to update endpoint URLs, and relies on admin CLI actions backed by wrangler d1 execute for enable or disable state.

For this slice, RFQ server validation for create_vault and close_vault is sufficient when the RFQ server itself broadcasts the transaction, verifies the transaction succeeded against the configured OTP_PACKAGE_ID, and only trusts buyer-vault results emitted from that package when deriving vault_id, owner_address, and quote_coin_type for D1 writes.

Maker Dashboard vault listing must read vault records and configuration from the database, while balances are read from chain on the web app side.

A makers_vaults table must be created using RFQ server migrations. Hidden Maker Dashboard means there is no visible navigation link to it anywhere in the web app. The list of supported coins is hardcoded on the server in a server configuration file and should be created if it does not exist.
acceptance_criteria:
- A maker can open the hidden Maker Dashboard and see their vaults, on-chain balances, enabled or disabled status, and current quote and order endpoint URLs
- A maker can create a vault for a supported QuoteCoin by submitting a wallet-signed create_vault transaction and registering the successful digest with RFQ/API
- A maker can deposit or withdraw QuoteCoin for a vault they own using wallet-signed on-chain transactions from the web app
- A maker can update quote endpoint URL and order endpoint URL for a specific maker vault through maker API flows with no admin UI required
- Server admin can enable or disable a specific maker vault without any UI requirement by using CLI actions backed by wrangler d1 execute
- RFQ/API persists maker vault metadata and configuration according to SPEC.md makers_vaults table fields, creates the makers_vaults table through migrations, stores the id, owner, and quote_coin_type fields of the on-chain BuyerVault struct, and marks a vault soft-deleted and disabled after a successful close_vault digest is submitted
- Closed or soft-deleted vaults cannot be edited or used by makers
- Hidden Maker Dashboard means there is no visible navigation link to it anywhere in the web app
- The list of supported coins is hardcoded on the server in a server configuration file and that file must be created if it does not already exist
- Balance reads for maker vaults happen on the web app side by reading from chain
- Quote/order API verification and signature verification are not required in this task and are explicitly deferred to future tasks
notes:
Clarified and unblocked on 2026-06-19: 1) request/response shapes for maker wallet flow endpoints may be designed in implementation as the simplest workable contract, 2) server-side validation is strong enough for this slice when RFQ itself broadcasts these transactions and verifies successful receipts against configured OTP_PACKAGE_ID before trusting create/close results, 3) Maker Dashboard reads vault records from DB and balances from chain.
Handoff 2026-06-19: Partial maker dashboard implementation is in apps/web only and is intentionally NOT ready to close. Current web changes hide maker routes from primary nav, restore a hidden maker shell with /maker, /maker/vaults, and /maker/positions, add RFQ + on-chain vault read path, add endpoint edit form with wallet-signed owner proof, and add close-digest submission UI. Validation completed: pnpm --dir apps/web test, pnpm --dir apps/web build, pnpm --dir apps/rfq-server test. Review sub-agent found remaining gaps that the next run must continue: 1) maker positions tab is still placeholder copy only and must load actual open positions, ITM/OTM state, required settlement funds, settlement readiness, and settlement history per SPEC.md; 2) vault dashboard still lacks create_vault and deposit/withdraw flows from the dashboard; 3) close_vault UX still only submits a pasted digest and does not yet drive the full sign/broadcast flow from the dashboard; 4) tests still cover route shape and rendered controls only, not the missing transaction/data flows. Working tree note: do not commit AGENTS.md, .beads/issues.jsonl, or tmp-shot artifacts; they are unrelated/runtime artifacts. Resume from current apps/web changes rather than restarting from scratch.
Breakdown 2026-06-19: remaining work split into c02.1 create-vault vertical, c02.2 funding vertical, c02.3 close-vault vertical, and c02.4 positions dashboard vertical. Continue with c02.1 first; c02.2 and c02.3 depend on it, while c02.4 is independent.

title: Review SPEC vs options contract spec conflicts
description: Why this exists: the product spec in SPEC.md needs to be checked against spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md for contradictions that could mislead implementation. What needs to be done: compare both documents, identify direct conflicts, rank them by severity, and report concrete sections involved.
close_reason: Completed spec conflict review

title: options: implement BuyerVault lifecycle
description:
## What to build

Implement the shared BuyerVault<QuoteCoin> lifecycle so a buyer can create one or more vaults, fund a vault, and withdraw its currently available balance. Each vault records its owner; creation, deposits, and withdrawals emit the specification events.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Any wallet can create multiple shared BuyerVault<QuoteCoin> objects, each recording ctx.sender() as owner and emitting BuyerVaultCreated with vault id, owner, and quote coin type.
- [ ] The owner can deposit Coin<QuoteCoin>, increasing the vault balance and emitting BuyerVaultDeposited with the deposited amount.
- [ ] Only the owner can deposit or withdraw; withdrawal is limited to the available balance and emits BuyerVaultWithdrawn with the withdrawn amount.
- [ ] Move tests cover successful lifecycle operations, multiple vaults for one owner/coin, unauthorized access, and insufficient-balance withdrawal.
- [ ] sui move test passes.
close_reason: Implemented shared BuyerVault creation, owner-only deposits and withdrawals, specified events, and full lifecycle tests.

title: options: implement market uniqueness and per-market pause
description: Implement the latest Market requirements from spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md. Market creation must reject duplicates with the same OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin combination. Admin pause authority must apply per market, and paused-market restrictions must be enforced for series creation and underwriting. The protocol may additionally expose a helper that pauses all markets in one transaction, but that helper is optional.
acceptance_criteria: Creating a duplicate OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin market aborts; distinct combinations remain creatable, including different wrapped BaseCoin types; an admin can pause and unpause an individual market; pausing one market does not pause another; series creation and underwriting abort for a paused market; existing allowed operations retain the specification's pause behavior; Move tests cover duplicate rejection and market-isolated pause behavior; sui move test passes.
close_reason: Implemented market uniqueness and verified isolated per-market pause behavior

title: spec: define per-market pause and duplicate market prevention
description: Update spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md to require per-market admin pause, optionally allow pausing all markets in one transaction, and require duplicate-market rejection for identical OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin combinations.
acceptance_criteria: Market creation rejects duplicate OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin combinations; pause is explicitly per-market and admin controlled; protocol-wide batch pause is explicitly optional.
close_reason: Updated the specification with per-market pause semantics, optional all-market pause batching, and duplicate market rejection.

title: Review codebase architecture depth
description: Surface architectural friction and deepening opportunities in a temp HTML report, using project domain vocabulary and architecture vocabulary.
close_reason: Generated temp architecture review report and opened it for review

title: Add options full lifecycle smoke test
description: Add a single Move test covering market creation, series creation, seller underwriting, ITM expiry finalization, buyer exercise, seller settlement, and series closure in one flow.
close_reason: Added full options call lifecycle smoke test and verified with sui move test.

title: Validate options end-to-end lifecycle
description: Manually validate or add a single Move spec test covering admin/operator market and series creation, seller underwriting with buyer order signature, expiry finalization ITM, buyer exercise, and operator settlement for the options package.
close_reason: Validated options package lifecycle with sui move test; full suite passed, with signed order and operator-role gaps filed separately.

title: Address expiry adapter review comments
description: Review feedback requests Pyth canonical oracle validation, removal of unnecessary payload hash Series storage, and a clearer single-series finalization helper shape.
close_reason: Addressed expiry adapter review comments

title: Document permissioned Pyth expiry finalization MVP
description: Update the options smart contract spec to define the MVP expiry price finalization design: permissioned Pyth unverifiable adapter, ExpiryPrice hot potato, and fixed-arity batch finalization helpers.
close_reason: Updated OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md with permissioned Pyth unverifiable expiry finalization, ExpiryPrice hot potato, and fixed-arity batch helpers.

title: Migrate Long quantity to Balance-backed accounting
description: OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md requires Long token quantity as Sui::Balance. Evaluate and implement migrating Long.quantity from u64 to Sui::Balance if it makes the Long token module design better. If Balance worsens the Long token design, defer this task and document the explanation for why Long.quantity as Sui::Balance is worse than the current u64 design.
notes: Deferred by automation on 2026-06-16: OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md requires Long.quantity as Sui::Balance, but the current design mints Long claim quantity while retaining actual BaseCoin/QuoteCoin collateral in the Series CollateralPool. A Sui Balance<T> is an actual coin-balance resource, so making Long.quantity a Balance<BaseCoin> needs a clarified accounting design: either Long holds real collateral balance, which conflicts with pool custody, or the protocol creates synthetic balances, which risks coin supply/resource semantics. Clarify whether the spec means Balance-backed real coin custody, a custom accounting balance type, or keep u64 claim quantity.
close_reason: Rejected Balance-backed Long quantity. Long.quantity is an option claim amount, while sui::balance::Balance<T> represents real coin supply/resource accounting. Using Balance for Long would imply coin/token supply semantics or collateral custody on the Long object, which conflicts with the current design where BaseCoin/QuoteCoin collateral stays in the Series CollateralPool. Keep Long.quantity as u64 unless Longs are intentionally redesigned as separate fungible Sui assets.

title: Extract Long token module
description: Move Long token object mechanics into a dedicated Move module while keeping pricing, exercise windows, claim pools, and seller accounting in core options modules.
close_reason: Completed Long token module extraction

title: Enforce 8 hour minimum time to expiry for options
description: Update the options spec and Move implementation so series creation and underwriting are rejected when expiry is within 8 hours.
close_reason: Implemented 8 hour minimum time-to-expiry rule in spec and series creation tests/code.

title: Parameterize market test fixture
description: Update create_market_fixture to accept market creation arguments and add coverage that multiple markets with distinct base/quote/collateral combinations can be created.
close_reason: Implemented parameterized market fixture and added multiple-market creation coverage.

title: Remove SPEC contract contradictions
description: Remove parts of SPEC.md that conflict with spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md so the product spec delegates canonical on-chain behavior to the smart-contract spec.
close_reason: Removed SPEC.md content that contradicted OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md and delegated canonical on-chain mechanics to the smart-contract spec.

title: Recovery after settlement
description:
## What to build

Implement admin recovery for closed series using this rule exactly:

"Rounding dust MUST remain in the internal `CollateralPool` of the `Series` and MUST be recoverable only through admin recovery after `series.state == Closed` and `now >= series.exception_window_end_ms` and `now >= series.exercise_window_end_ms` and all seller payouts, manual exercises for given `Series` are fully accounted and settled, only the remaining unreserved balance may be recovered."

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Admin recovery is limited to only the remaining unreserved balance of the series internal `CollateralPool`.
- [ ] Recovery is allowed only after `series.state == Closed`.
- [ ] Recovery is allowed only after `now >= series.exception_window_end_ms` and `now >= series.exercise_window_end_ms`.
- [ ] Recovery is allowed only after all seller payouts and manual exercises for the given `Series` are fully accounted and settled.
- [ ] Recovery emits the required admin recovery event with asset, amount, recipient, and reason code.
close_reason: Implemented admin recovery for closed-series rounding dust with tests

title: Manual exercise and seller settlement
description:
## What to build

Implement holder-initiated manual physical exercise and permissionless seller settlement for no-exercise and fully exercised series.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Manual exercise consumes the whole matching long token, enforces the finalized ITM exercise window, validates payment, and transfers the correct physical settlement asset.
- [ ] Exercise accounting records quantities and proceeds for both calls and puts without overdrawing the collateral pool.
- [ ] Permissionless batched seller settlement pays sellers directly for ATM/OTM and fully exercised ITM outcomes, closes settled vault records, and closes the series when complete.

## Blocked by

- Call and put underwriting
- Price finalization and phase derivation
close_reason: Implemented manual exercise and seller settlement with tests

title: Price finalization and phase derivation
description:
## What to build

Finalize immutable expiry prices through the permissioned admin/operator price finalization adapter and expose deterministic phase and moneyness behavior for option series.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Price finalization is available after expiry through the admin/operator adapter, validates oracle identity/feed id/publish time/positive price, and stores the price immutably.
- [ ] Batch finalization only accepts series from the same market.
- [ ] Derived phase and ITM/ATM/OTM behavior matches the spec for calls, puts, manual exercise, exception window, partial settlement, and full settlement.

## Blocked by

- Expiry price finalization adapter
- Series creation with collateral pool
notes: Deferred by automation on 2026-06-16: implementation and tests now cover after-expiry finalization, immutable price storage, oracle/feed/publish/positive price checks, same-market batch rejection, and call/put phase boundaries. Resolution on 2026-06-16: preserve the permissioned admin/operator adapter described in spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md; acceptance was updated from permissionless finalization to admin/operator adapter finalization.
close_reason: Completed: implementation and tests satisfy clarified admin-cap price finalization and phase derivation acceptance criteria.

title: Call and put underwriting
description:
## What to build

Support fully collateralized call and put underwriting that creates transferable long option tokens, records seller short obligations, and handles premium and fee settlement.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Call underwriting deposits BaseCoin collateral and put underwriting deposits QuoteCoin collateral using solvency-favoring strike payment rounding.
- [ ] Underwriting mints the correct long token to the buyer, updates one seller vault record per seller/series, and increases series short quantity.
- [ ] Premium, operational fee, fee recipient, and optional max-fee checks are enforced atomically.
- [ ] Long tokens support split and join only for identical option class and series fields.

## Blocked by

- Series creation with collateral pool
close_reason: Implemented fully collateralized call/put underwriting with long tokens, seller vault accounting, premium/fee handling, and tests.

title: Market admin and pause lifecycle
description:
## What to build

Implement the shared Market lifecycle with admin authority, supported asset identity, and global pause behavior for the options protocol.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Markets store the OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin combination, oracle metadata, decimals, strike scale, supported coin types, admin authority, and pause state.
- [ ] Unsupported coin types are rejected by market-scoped operations introduced in this slice.
- [ ] Pause and unpause require admin authority and emit the required events.

## Blocked by

- Sui Move project lean setup
close_reason: Implemented shared market lifecycle with admin capability, coin-type guards, pause/unpause events, and TDD coverage.

title: Series creation with collateral pool
description:
## What to build

Allow anyone to create a valid option series for an existing market, with exactly one isolated collateral pool for that series.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Series creation rejects invalid option type, zero strike, past expiry, unsupported market coin types, and duplicate market/type/strike/expiry tuples.
- [ ] Each created series references exactly one collateral pool for that series and emits series creation data.
- [ ] Series state and timing fields support the spec's derived expiry phases without adding extra stored phase states.

## Blocked by

- Market admin and pause lifecycle
close_reason: Implemented series creation with collateral pool and TDD coverage

title: Expiry price finalization adapter
description:
## What to build

Decide and encode the contract-facing expiry price finalization adapter shape for permissionless option series finalization after expiry.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The accepted oracle source, proof/payload shape, and validation responsibilities are recorded clearly enough for implementation.
- [ ] The adapter design supports market oracle name/feed id checks, positive price checks, publish time at or after expiry, and batch finalization by market.
- [ ] The design leaves exercise blocked when no valid bounded expiry price can be finalized.

## Blocked by

- Sui Move project lean setup
notes:
Deferred for 36h: needs human/product decision before implementation. Missing requirements: accepted oracle source, proof/payload shape, and exact validation responsibility boundary for the expiry price adapter.
The option smart contract specification has been updated to address finalization requirements issues. Take anohter look at the task and the spec, update this task if the spec now answers all the questions and requiments are clear, and if the requirement, spec, task is good to be implemented then work on it.
close_reason: Implemented Pyth unverifiable expiry price adapter and fixed-arity series finalization with tests

title: Sui Move project lean setup
description:
## What to build

Create the lean Sui Move package foundation for the options smart contract work so later slices can add production behavior without reworking project structure.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The repository has a minimal Sui Move package configured for the options protocol.
- [ ] The package builds with the chosen Sui toolchain and contains only scaffold-level modules/tests needed by follow-on slices.
- [ ] The package metadata or local docs make the intended build/test command clear for AFK agents.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
notes: Implemented lean packages/options Sui Move scaffold with Move.toml package name options_trading_protocol, package_info source module, package_info unit test, Move.lock, and README build/test commands. Final validation passed: sui move test and sui move build both complete successfully from packages/options using Sui 1.72.1.
close_reason: Completed lean Sui Move package scaffold; sui move test and sui move build pass for packages/options.

title: Configure max operational fee per options contract spec
description: Add mechanics to configure and enforce max_operational_fee according to OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md. The spec allows a market admin-configured maximum fee basis points; if configured, underwriting must reject fees above that maximum.

title: Deduplicate underwriting cutoff wording in options spec
description: Define the minimum underwriting time to expiry once in the options smart contract spec and reference it from series creation and underwriting requirements.
close_reason: Defined minimum underwriting time to expiry once and referenced it from series creation and underwriting requirements.

title: Recovery after partial ITM settlement
description:
## What to build

Allow admin recovery after partial ITM settlement where exercised and unexercised obligations were paid through mixed seller settlement.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Recovery accounts for mixed exercise proceeds and returned collateral before identifying excess balances.
- [ ] Recovery cannot withdraw anything reserved for unsettled seller vaults or active accounting obligations.
- [ ] Dust and excess recovery remains delayed, evented, and checked against collateral pool accounting.

## Blocked by

- Partial ITM settlement after missed exception

title: Recovery for exercise-by-exception and claim-poll settlement path
description:
## What to build

Allow admin recovery for series settled through exercise-by-exception while preserving all claim pool balances owed to late long holders.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Recovery can clean up excess or dust in old series/collateral objects after claim-pool settlement is complete.
- [ ] Recovery cannot withdraw claim-pool balances reserved for unclaimed matching long tokens.
- [ ] Closed or depleted claim-pool cleanup is supported where storage can be safely reclaimed.

## Blocked by

- Exercise-by-exception and claim pools

title: Partial ITM settlement after missed exception
description:
## What to build

Settle ITM series after the exception window when some longs were manually exercised but exercise-by-exception did not complete.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] After the exception window, remaining unexercised ITM longs are no longer exercisable or claimable when exercise-by-exception did not complete.
- [ ] Seller settlement pays the exercised portion from exercise proceeds and returns original collateral for the unexercised portion.
- [ ] Settlement remains permissionless, batched, pro-rata by seller short quantity, and dust-safe.

## Blocked by

- Manual exercise and seller settlement
- Exercise-by-exception and claim pools

title: Exercise-by-exception and claim pools
description:
## What to build

Implement permissionless exercise-by-exception for remaining ITM longs and create claim pools that late holders can redeem against by burning matching long tokens.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] During the exception window, anyone can settle the remaining unexercised ITM quantity for calls and puts when required seller payment and minimum net claim constraints are satisfied.
- [ ] Net proceeds for late holders are deposited into a claim pool only when non-zero, and the operation records exercise-by-exception accounting.
- [ ] Claim pool redemption burns a matching long token, pays a solvency-rounded pro-rata share, handles final rounding dust safely, and does not require loading old series or seller vault records.

## Blocked by

- Price finalization and phase derivation
- Flash-loan exercise composability

title: Flash-loan exercise composability
description:
## What to build

Make exercise paths composable inside Sui PTBs so callers can combine exercise proceeds with external flash-loan, swap, and repayment routes atomically.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Core exercise functions expose PTB-friendly return values or wrappers that allow proceeds to be routed before final transfer.
- [ ] The options contract does not hard-code any lending protocol, DEX, swap route, or provider-specific dependency.
- [ ] Tests or examples demonstrate that borrow/exercise/repay-style composition can be expressed atomically around the core exercise path.

## Blocked by

- Manual exercise and seller settlement
